home.hero_statement: The #1 Chemistry academic platform combining interactive simulations and online competition.

journey.title: Learning Journey
journey.desc: From the most basic concepts of Grade 8 Chemistry to advanced topics in Grade 12. Content is digitized into interactive lessons that sharpen both theory and practice.
journey.desc_long: From the most basic concepts of Grade 8 Chemistry to advanced topics in Grade 12. Content is digitized into interactive lessons that sharpen both theory and practice.

lab.title: Virtual Lab
lab.desc: No real chemicals needed, no risk of explosions. Mix, observe color changes, precipitates, and gas evolution right on your screen. Supports hundreds of high school to advanced reactions.
lab.desc_long: Mix chemicals, observe color changes, precipitates, and gas evolution safely in 3D right on your screen.

arena.title: Battle Arena
arena.desc: Real-time ranking system. Create battle rooms with friends or find random matches. Quick-fire Chemistry questions to earn points and unlock exclusive avatars.
arena.desc_long: Real-time ranking system. Battle other students with quick-fire Chemistry questions, earn points, and climb the leaderboard.

library.title: Comprehensive Lookup
library.desc: A massive digital library paired with an interactive Periodic Table. Click any element to view electron configuration, energy levels, and detailed chemical properties.

map.title: Journey
map.desc: Embark on an RPG-style learning adventure. Gather achievements, unlock new stages, and showcase your chemistry prowess.

lessons.title: Interactive Lessons
lessons.desc: Theory presented through interactive slides, video illustrations, and quick quizzes to prepare you for exams.

about.title: Igniting the passion for <1/><2> Chemistry</2> in youth
about.description: Aurum is not just a learning platform, but a journey to explore the microscopic world of atoms and reactions. We believe knowledge truly comes alive when experienced and interacted with.

safety.title: Absolute Safety
safety.desc: 3D simulations help students practice dangerous reactions without real chemicals.

gamification.title: Learn Through Play
gamification.desc: Integrated gamification system with points and ranks makes learning engaging.

visualization.title: Visualization
visualization.desc: All abstract concepts are digitized into vivid and easy-to-understand images.

team_section.title: Expert Team from KTH Academy
team_section.description: We bring together passionate Chemistry lecturers and professional software engineers. With the mantra "Digitalizing education is the key to the future", we constantly strive to perfect every lesson and simulation.

contact.title: Contact our <1/> Founding Team
contact.description: We are always happy to hear your feedback, comments, or collaboration requests. Don't hesitate to reach out through our official channels below.

terms.title: Terms & <1/> Privacy Policy

acceptance.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
acceptance.p1: By accessing and using Aurum Chemistry Odyssey, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Use. If you do not agree with any part of these terms, please refrain from using our services.
acceptance.p2: We reserve the right to modify or update these terms at any time without prior notice. Your continued use of the platform after changes are posted constitutes your acceptance of the new terms.

accounts.title: 2. User Accounts
accounts.p1: To access certain features, you may need to register for an account. You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account information and password. All activities that occur under your account are your responsibility.
accounts.p2: Sharing accounts, using cheating tools in the arena, or intentionally disrupting the community is strictly prohibited. We reserve the right to suspend accounts that violate these rules without notice.

intellectual.title: 3. Intellectual Property
intellectual.p1: All content on Aurum, including but not limited to lectures, 3D simulations, images, and source code, is owned by Aurum Chemistry Academy or its licensors.
intellectual.p2: Any unauthorized copying, distribution, or commercialization of the content without our prior written consent is strictly prohibited and may result in legal action.

privacy.title: 4. Privacy Policy
privacy.p1: We are committed to protecting your personal information. Collected data (such as name, email, and learning progress) is used solely to personalize your learning experience and improve our service quality.
privacy.p2: We never sell or share your personal information with third parties for marketing purposes without your explicit consent.

liability.title: 5. Limitation of Liability
liability.p1: Lab simulations and knowledge provided on this platform are for educational and reference purposes only. Users should not perform dangerous experiments in real life without expert supervision.
liability.p2: We are not liable for any direct or indirect damages resulting from the use of information or incorrect practice of instructions found on the platform.

terms.footer_question: Have questions about the terms?<1/>Please <contact>Contact Us</contact>.

quick_guide.title: Quick Lookup
quick_guide.desc: Click elements to view 3D atomic simulations

H.name: Hydrogen
H.desc: The lightest and most abundant element in the universe. A primary component of water.
facts: Makes up 75% of the universe's mass
Solar energy comes from hydrogen fusion
Can become metallic at extreme pressure

He.name: Helium
He.desc: Inert gas, second lightest after Hydrogen. Discovered on the Sun before being found on Earth.
facts: Discovered on the Sun before Earth
Name from Helios - Greek sun god

Li.name: Lithium
Li.desc: Lightest alkali metal, soft and silvery. Reacts strongly with water.

Be.name: Beryllium
Be.desc: Light alkaline earth metal with high hardness and melting point.
facts: Does not react with water at room temp

B.name: Boron
B.desc: Hard, black metalloid, semiconductor. Essential for plants.
facts: Primary component of Pyrex heat-resistant glass
Pure boron is very hard, second only to diamond
Can absorb neutrons in nuclear reactors

C.name: Carbon
C.desc: Basic element of life, can form over 10 million different compounds.
facts: Diamond and graphite are both pure carbon
Basis of all life on Earth

N.name: Nitrogen
N.desc: Gas making up 78% of Earth's atmosphere. Essential for protein and DNA.
facts: N≡N triple bond is very stable
Used for cooling and food preservation
Essential for DNA, RNA, and protein

O.name: Oxygen
O.desc: Essential for respiration and combustion. Third most abundant element in the universe.

F.name: Fluorine
F.desc: Most reactive halogen, pale yellow gas, extremely toxic and corrosive.
facts: Reacts with almost everything, including noble gases

Ne.name: Neon
Ne.desc: Noble gas, colorless, odorless, used in neon signs.
facts: Creates signature orange-red light in neon lamps
Fifth most abundant noble gas in the universe
Does not react with any element

Na.name: Sodium
Na.desc: Soft metal, reacts strongly with water creating a yellow flame.

Mg.name: Magnesium
Mg.desc: Light metal, burns brightly with a blinding white flame.
facts: Essential for over 300 enzymes in the body

Al.name: Aluminum
Al.desc: Light metal, corrosion-resistant, widely used in daily life.
facts: Most abundant metal in Earth's crust
Aluminum cans can be recycled infinitely
Washington Monument cap is made of aluminum

Si.name: Silicon
Si.desc: Metalloid, 2nd most abundant element in Earth's crust, basis of electronic technology.
facts: Silicon Valley is named after it

P.name: Phosphorus
P.desc: Nonmetal with multiple allotropes, essential for DNA and ATP.
facts: White phosphorus glows in the dark
Essential for DNA, RNA and ATP
Red phosphorus is safe, used in matches

S.name: Sulfur
S.desc: Yellow nonmetal, smells like rotten eggs when combined with hydrogen.
facts: Rotten egg smell is H₂S containing sulfur
Used for sulfuric acid - #1 industrial chemical
Essential for methionine and cysteine amino acids

Cl.name: Chlorine
Cl.desc: Greenish-yellow gas, toxic, pungent odor. Used for water disinfection.
facts: Used as chemical weapon in WWI
Signature pungent odor like swimming pools

Ar.name: Argon
Ar.desc: Noble gas making up 0.93% of air, used in welding and light bulbs.
facts: Used in bulbs to prevent filament evaporation

K.name: Potassium
K.desc: Soft alkali metal, reacts violently with water.
facts: More explosive reaction with water than sodium
Can be cut with a knife due to softness
Essential for nerve and muscle function

Ca.name: Calcium
Ca.desc: Alkaline earth metal, primary component of bones and teeth.
facts: Makes up 1.5% of human body (bones, teeth)
Primary component of limestone and marble
Calcium metal can be cut with a knife

Fe.name: Iron
Fe.desc: Most common and important metal for industry and biology.
facts: Earth's core is mostly molten iron

Cu.name: Copper
Cu.desc: Reddish metal, second best conductor of electricity and heat after silver.
facts: Statue of Liberty is coated in copper

Zn.name: Zinc
Zn.desc: Bluish-grey metal, used for galvanizing iron against rust and in batteries.

Ag.name: Silver
Ag.desc: Precious metal with the highest electrical and thermal conductivity.

Au.name: Gold
Au.desc: Most ductile and malleable precious metal, does not tarnish.
facts: One ounce can be drawn into 50 miles of wire
Basis of global financial systems for centuries

status.empty_desc: Explore the magical world of chemistry right now

8.title: Part 1
8.name: The Awakening Beginning
8.story: Enter the primal molecular world, where everything begins. Your mission is to understand the nature of atoms and master the invisible language of Chemistry.

9.title: Part 2
9.name: The Transformation Journey
9.story: Explosive reactions and constantly changing substances. Unlock the secrets of chemical reactions and capture the power of interaction between elements.

10.title: Part 3
10.name: The Macro Leap
10.story: Deep dive into dynamics and periodic table structure. You will have to get used to the balance, heat, and momentum that govern the entire material universe.

11.title: Part 4
11.name: The Organic Matrix
11.story: Lost in the labyrinth of Carbon atoms. Here, life forms from complex and mysterious circuits. You must find the rules that create all life.

12.title: Part 5
12.name: Reaching Evolution Peak
12.story: The final frontier for alchemy masters. Overcome electrochemical challenges, discover polymer materials and fusion to complete the destiny of creating a new universe.
